    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  try {\n    const { code, state: masjidId } = await request.json();\n\n    if (!code || !masjidId) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Code and masjidId are required\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Exchange code for tokens\n    const { tokens } = await oauth2Client.getToken({\n      code,\n      redirect_uri: process.env.GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI + '/api/google/calendar/callback',\n    });\n    oauth2Client.setCredentials(tokens);\n\n\n    // Get the calendar ID\n    const calendar = google.calendar({ version: \"v3\", auth: oauth2Client });\n    // Get a the user's profile picture\n    const user = google.oauth2({ version: \"v2\", auth: oauth2Client });\n    const userInfo = await user.userinfo.get();\n    const profilePicture = userInfo.data.picture;\n    const calendarList = await calendar.calendarList.list();\n    const primaryCalendar = calendarList.data.items?.find(\n      (item) =\u003e item.primary\n    );\n\n    if (!primaryCalendar?.id) {\n      throw new Error(\"No primary calendar found\");\n    }\n\n    console.log(primaryCalendar, user, profilePicture);\n\n    // Update masjid with calendar ID and credentials\n    await prisma.masjid.update({\n      where: { id: masjidId },\n      data: {\n        googleCalendarId: primaryCalendar.id,\n        googleCalendarCredentials: tokens,\n        googleCalendarPfp: profilePicture,\n      },\n    });\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Error handling Google OAuth callback:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to handle OAuth callback\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}",

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  try {\n    const { code, state: masjidId } = await request.json();\n\n    if (!code || !masjidId) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Code and masjidId are required\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Exchange code for tokens\n    const { tokens } = await oauth2Client.getToken({\n      code,\n      redirect_uri: process.env.GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI + '/api/google/calendar/callback',\n    });\n    oauth2Client.setCredentials(tokens);\n\n\n    // Get the calendar ID\n    const calendar = google.calendar({ version: \"v3\", auth: oauth2Client });\n    // Get a the user's profile picture\n    const user = google.oauth2({ version: \"v2\", auth: oauth2Client });\n    const userInfo = await user.userinfo.get();\n    const profilePicture = userInfo.data.picture;\n    const calendarList = await calendar.calendarList.list();\n    const primaryCalendar = calendarList.data.items?.find(\n      (item) =\u003e item.primary\n    );\n\n    if (!primaryCalendar?.id) {\n      throw new Error(\"No primary calendar found\");\n    }\n\n    console.log(primaryCalendar, user, profilePicture);\n\n    // Update masjid with calendar ID and credentials\n    await prisma.masjid.update({\n      where: { id: masjidId },\n      data: {\n        googleCalendarId: primaryCalendar.id,\n        googleCalendarCredentials: tokens,\n        googleCalendarPfp: profilePicture,\n      },\n    });\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Error handling Google OAuth callback:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to handle OAuth callback\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}",

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="codebase_search"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **codebase_search**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent**_

Here’s what I found after reviewing your codebase:

---

## **Root Cause: Server-Only Functions Used on the Client**

- Your `getUserMasjid` and `getEvents` functions **directly use Prisma** and are marked `"use server"`.
- These functions **cannot be called from the client** (i.e., inside `useEffect` or any client component). When you try, they will hang or silently fail, and no fetch will happen in the browser.

---

## **How to Fix**

### 1. **Create API Routes for Data Fetching**
- Move your data fetching logic (e.g., Prisma queries) into API routes under `/app/api/`.
- In your client components, use `fetch('/api/your-endpoint')` to get data.

### 2. **Update Client Components**
- In your client components (like `EventsPage`), use `fetch` to call your new API endpoints instead of calling server functions directly.

---

## **Example Fix for `getUserMasjid`**

**Create an API route:**  
`/app/api/masjid/route.ts`
```ts
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { getUserMasjid } from "@/lib/actions/masjid";

export async function GET() {
  const masjid = await getUserMasjid();
  return NextResponse.json(masjid);
}
```

**In your client component:**
```js
async function fetchUserMasjid() {
  const res = await fetch('/api/masjid');
  return await res.json();
}
```
And use `fetchUserMasjid()` in your `useEffect`.

---

## **Example Fix for `getEvents`**

**Create an API route:**  
`/app/api/events/route.ts`
```ts
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { getEvents } from "@/lib/actions/events";

export async function GET(request: Request) {
  const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
  const masjidId = searchParams.get("masjidId");
  if (!masjidId) return NextResponse.json([]);
  const events = await getEvents(masjidId);
  return NextResponse.json(events);
}
```

**In your client component:**
```js
async function fetchEvents(masjidId) {
  const res = await fetch(`/api/events?masjidId=${masjidId}`);
  return await res.json();
}
```
And use `fetchEvents(masjidId)` in your `useEffect`.

---

## **Summary of Steps to Take**

1. **Do not call server-only functions from the client.**
2. **Move all Prisma/data logic to API routes.**
3. **Call those API routes from your client components using `fetch`.**
4. **Update your state with the fetched data.**

---

Would you like me to generate the API route files and update your client code to use them?  
This will get your dashboard and calendar working again!

_**Agent (2025-05-06 10:07:33Z)**_

## **Plan**

1. **Create API routes** for `/api/masjid` and `/api/events` that:
   - Only return data if the user is authenticated.
   - Only return masjid/events data if the user has access to that masjid.

## **Step 1: Create `/app/api/masjid/route.ts`**

This route will:
- Get the current user (using your existing `getUser` or session logic).
- Return the masjid only if the user is the owner.



_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file** • Edit file: app/api/masjid/route.ts</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 15, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,15 @@
- 
+ import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
+ import { getUser } from "@/lib/actions/user";
+ import { getUserMasjid } from "@/lib/actions/masjid";
+ 
+ export async function GET() {
+   const user = await getUser();
+   if (!user) {
+     return NextResponse.json({ error: true, message: "Not authenticated" }, { status: 401 });
+   }
+   const masjid = await getUserMasjid();
+   if (!masjid || masjid.ownerId !== user.id) {
+     return NextResponse.json({ error: true, message: "No masjid found or not authorized" }, { status: 403 });
+   }
+   return NextResponse.json(masjid);
+ } 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (2025-05-06 10:07:33Z)**_

Great idea! A custom event list for mobile will be much more user-friendly and performant than trying to squeeze FullCalendar into a small screen.

**Here’s the plan:**
1. **On mobile:**  
   - Render a custom component that:
     - Lets the user pick a month and week.
     - Lists all events for the selected week in a card format.
     - Has a clean, modern design.
2. **On desktop/tablet:**  
   - Keep using FullCalendar as before.

---

**Let’s proceed step by step:**

- I’ll create a new `MobileEventList` component.
- I’ll update `calendar-view.tsx` to use this component on mobile.

Ready? Let’s start by creating the custom mobile event list!

_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file** • Edit file: components/dashboard/mobile-event-list.tsx</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 73, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,73 @@
- 
+ import { useState, useMemo } from "react";
+ import { format, startOfWeek, endOfWeek, isWithinInterval, addWeeks, subWeeks, addMonths, subMonths, startOfMonth, endOfMonth } from "date-fns";
+ import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
+ import { Card, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card";
+ import { CalendarIcon, Clock, MapPin, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from "lucide-react";
+ 
+ export function MobileEventList({ events }: { events: any[] }) {
+   const [currentDate, setCurrentDate] = useState(new Date());
+ 
+   // Calculate current week range
+   const weekStart = startOfWeek(currentDate, { weekStartsOn: 0 });
+   const weekEnd = endOfWeek(currentDate, { weekStartsOn: 0 });
+ 
+   // Filter events for this week
+   const weekEvents = useMemo(() =&gt; {
+     return events.filter(event =&gt; {
+       const eventDate = new Date(event.date);
+       return isWithinInterval(eventDate, { start: weekStart, end: weekEnd });
+     });
+   }, [events, weekStart, weekEnd]);
+ 
+   // Handlers for week/month navigation
+   const prevWeek = () =&gt; setCurrentDate(subWeeks(currentDate, 1));
+   const nextWeek = () =&gt; setCurrentDate(addWeeks(currentDate, 1));
+   const prevMonth = () =&gt; setCurrentDate(subMonths(currentDate, 1));
+   const nextMonth = () =&gt; setCurrentDate(addMonths(currentDate, 1));
+ 
+   // Month and week display
+   const monthLabel = format(currentDate, "MMMM yyyy");
+   const weekLabel = &#96;${format(weekStart, "MMM d")} - ${format(weekEnd, "MMM d")}&#96;;
+ 
+   return (
+     &lt;div className="w-full"&gt;
+       &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-2 gap-2"&gt;
+         &lt;Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={prevMonth}&gt;&lt;ChevronLeft /&gt;&lt;/Button&gt;
+         &lt;span className="font-semibold text-lg"&gt;{monthLabel}&lt;/span&gt;
+         &lt;Button variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={nextMonth}&gt;&lt;ChevronRight /&gt;&lt;/Button&gt;
+       &lt;/div&gt;
+       &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4 gap-2"&gt;
+         &lt;Button variant="outline" size="sm" onClick={prevWeek}&gt;Previous Week&lt;/Button&gt;
+         &lt;span className="font-medium text-base"&gt;{weekLabel}&lt;/span&gt;
+         &lt;Button variant="outline" size="sm" onClick={nextWeek}&gt;Next Week&lt;/Button&gt;
+       &lt;/div&gt;
+       {weekEvents.length === 0 ? (
+         &lt;div className="text-center text-muted-foreground py-8"&gt;
+           &lt;CalendarIcon className="mx-auto mb-2 h-8 w-8 text-[#550C18]/50" /&gt;
+           &lt;div&gt;No events this week.&lt;/div&gt;
+         &lt;/div&gt;
+       ) : (
+         &lt;div className="space-y-4"&gt;
+           {weekEvents.map(event =&gt; (
+             &lt;Card key={event.id} className="border-[#550C18]/20 shadow-sm"&gt;
+               &lt;CardContent className="py-4 px-3"&gt;
+                 &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-2"&gt;
+                   &lt;span className="font-semibold text-[#550C18] text-lg"&gt;{event.title}&lt;/span&gt;
+                   &lt;span className="ml-auto text-xs bg-[#550C18]/10 text-[#550C18] rounded px-2 py-0.5"&gt;{format(new Date(event.date), "EEE, MMM d")}&lt;/span&gt;
+                 &lt;/div&gt;
+                 &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-[#3A3A3A]/80 mb-1"&gt;
+                   &lt;Clock className="h-4 w-4" /&gt;
+                   &lt;span&gt;{format(new Date(event.timeStart), "h:mm a")} - {format(new Date(event.timeEnd), "h:mm a")}&lt;/span&gt;
+                 &lt;/div&gt;
+                 &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-[#3A3A3A]/80"&gt;
+                   &lt;MapPin className="h-4 w-4" /&gt;
+                   &lt;span&gt;{event.location}&lt;/span&gt;
+                 &lt;/div&gt;
+               &lt;/CardContent&gt;
+             &lt;/Card&gt;
+           ))}
+         &lt;/div&gt;
+       )}
+     &lt;/div&gt;
+   );
+ } 
```

</details>

</tool-use>
